title: Oil Holds Steady After Iran Signals End to Attacks on Israel
summary: Oil held steady, reversing an earlier rally, after Iranian media said the country’s armed forces had declared an end to the military operation against Israel after a day of hostilities between the two sides.

title: Treasuries Erase Drop in Oil-Driven Move Tied to Middle East War
summary: Treasuries erased declines that pushed the two-year note’s yield to a new 2026 high as the market responded to oil-price fluctuations caused by the US war on Iran.

title: Top Brazil Broker Tells Stock Traders: Don’t Just Buy the Index
summary: Investors should get pickier with Brazil stocks as economic growth is set to cool and the nation heads toward a polarized presidential vote, says XP Inc.

title: GE Sees Potential For More China Deals After Summit
summary: GE Aerospace remains optimistic that it can secure more aircraft-engine orders from China after President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping met in Beijing last month and manufacturing partner Boeing Co. came home an initial batch of commitments.

title: Woodford Sued by UK for Alleged Unauthorized Investment Advice
summary: Britain’s financial watchdog sued Neil Woodford, the once-renowned investment manager, and his firm over allegations that he’s providing regulated investment advice without authorization.

title: Stocks Gain as Buyers Step In After AI-Led Selloff: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, lifting stocks amid renewed enthusiasm over artificial intelligence and bets that a solid economy will keep powering Corporate America.

title: Latam Airlines CEO on Price Hikes, Taxation, Expansion
summary: Latam Airlines CEO Roberto Alvo says that high fuel prices could lead to capacity cuts and South American taxes are keeping some travelers out of the skies. Alvo spoke with Bloomberg's Guy Johnson on the sidelines of the IATA 2026 Conference. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: A Data Center Is a Bank
summary: Maybe we need universal basic tokens.

title: Schwab's Martin Says Fed Is Edging Toward a Rate Hike
summary: There's a case for the Federal Reserve to "hike right now,'' and even if policymakers aren't ready yet, the bar to action is definitely getting lower, according to Collin Martin at the Schwab Center for Financial Research. He speaks with Jonathan Ferro and Annmarie Hordern on Bloomberg Television's "Surveillance.'' (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Rebound From Selloff as Nvidia Leads Big-Tech Gains
summary: US stocks bounced back on Monday from the worst rout this year, as a selloff in technology stocks eased and traders assessed flaring tensions in the Middle East, which supported oil prices and energy shares.

title: Apple Investors Look for AI Overhaul to Power Next Leg of Gains
summary: Apple Inc. investors have spent nearly two years clamoring for the iPhone maker to make a big splash with artificial intelligence. Their wait may finally be coming to an end this week at the company’s annual Worldwide Developers Conference.

title: UK’s Crown Estate Plans to Resell Abandoned Wind Farm Site
summary: The British King’s commercial property and land manager is bringing back to market the rights to develop a major wind farm in the Irish Sea after the former developers of the site walked away last year.

title: European Stocks Swing on Mideast Tensions; Zealand Pharma Sinks
summary: European stocks swung between gains and losses in a volatile session as investors weighed conflicting signals from the Middle East.

title: Southwest COO on Strong Business Travel, Price Increases
summary: Southwest Airlines Chief Operating Officer Andrew Watterson says business travel has remained “incredibly strong” despite higher costs related to the War in Iran. He speaks with Lisa Abramowicz on the sidelines of the IATA Conference in Brazil. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Europe’s Imports of Diesel and Jet Fuel Shrivel as War Drags On
summary: Europe’s imports of diesel and jet fuel stagnated for a second month in May, piling pressure on regional energy supplies as the Iran war grinds on.

title: SpaceX Set for Historic IPO This Week
summary: Elon Musk wants to build the infrastructure for AI in space — and he's asking investors to help pay for it. The rocket, satellite and now AI company wants to raise around 75 billion dollars in what could become the largest IPO in history, funding everything from its Starship rocket to AI computing power and future data centers in orbit. Bloomberg Tech's Ed Ludlow takes a look at what's at stake. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fed Faces Rising Rate Hike Expectations, Schwab Center's Martin Says
summary: The bar for a Federal Reserve rate hike is falling as the job market remains robust in the face of stubborn price pressures, according to Collin Martin at the Schwab Center for Financial Research.

title: Chile’s Tren de Aragua Probe Revives Bank Secrecy Debate
summary: The June arrests of bank employees accused of ties to Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua criminal organization has reignited debate over Chile’s financial secrecy rules, which are among the strictest in the world.

title: Tate & Lyle Accepts Bid From Ingredion in Loss for London
summary: Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), in a move that marks the end of the UK company’s near-century on the London Stock Exchange.

title: Latest Houthi Shipping Threat Is Blunted by Thin Red Sea Traffic
summary: Transits into the Gulf of Aden remain far below levels seen back in 2023, when the militants lashed out at commercial shipping.

title: Peru Bonds Edge Lower as Election Result Hangs in the Balance
summary: Peruvian bonds edged lower as the country’s bitterly contested presidential election remained too close to call with the latest data showing conservative Keiko Fujimori holding the narrowest of leads over leftist Roberto Sánchez.

title: Copper Rebounds After Report Iran Has Ended Israel Strikes
summary: Copper climbed, recouping some of last week’s losses, as Iranian media reported that the country has ended a military operation against Israel.

title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh US-Iran Attempts to End Attacks
summary: Gold erased losses following a report that Iran had ended military operations against Israel, potentially removing a major obstacle to efforts to bring an end to the wider Middle East conflict.

title: US Premarket Movers for June 8, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.6% at 7:49 a.m. in New York, signaling a modest rebound for stocks after Friday’s slide.

title: Lebanon Emerges as Israeli Wedge Between Trump and Netanyahu
summary: From the beginning of the US-Iranian negotiations to end the war in the Middle East, Israel has repeatedly demanded its conflict with Lebanon’s Hezbollah be left out.

title: Shekel Flips From Best to Worst, Easing Pressure on Central Bank
summary: Israel’s shekel has swung from the world’s best performer to the worst in a matter of days, easing pressure on the central bank to stem a 14-month rally that’s hurting the country’s exporters.

title: Trump Says Iran, Israel Looking to Do ‘Immediate Ceasefire’
summary: US President Donald Trump said Israel and Iran are looking to agree to an immediate ceasefire following a series of strikes, while Iran’s central military command says the military operation against Israel has ended, according to semi-official Fars news agency. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Halt Selloff; Iran, Israel Exchange Missile Strikes | Bloomberg Brief 6/8/2026
summary: US stocks stage a modest rebound after Friday's selloff. Oil climbs after Iran and Israel trade missile attacks. Sikander Rashid of Brookfield speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie at London Tech Week on the firm's investment in AI infrastructure. (Source: Bloomberg)
